content: 2024-2025 state-of-the-art active learning agent memory systems can be structured as nested hierarchical agent communities, where each individual memory chunk (e.g. a stored interaction, observed pattern) acts as an independent curiosity-driven micro-agent that votes on whether the active learning core should prioritize retrieving it for unlabeled data querying. This replaces traditional fixed priority ranking of memory with emergent collective decision-making aligned to the agent's ongoing exploration goals.
